title: carlina graeca var. graeca
intro: Carlina graeca var. graeca, also known as the Greek hawk's beard, is a plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its tall stature, yellow flowers, and a distinctive beard-like appearance on its seeds. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Carlina graeca var. graeca include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, as well as its ability to soothe respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be consumed fresh or dried, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional.
